---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 804eaa1e522380ed29362039fdf92db7860f2878
findings:
  - { id: judged-dc7-schema-ratification-bypass, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-7 establishes that the diagram schema surface ratifies as one 02 amendment batch BEFORE implementation (the scoping-canvas round-5.4 pattern), and enumerates that batch's contents — the sweep artifact is not among them. Story dc-3 mints a new artifact schema (verdi.diagramsweep/v1 with covers, findings, sweep_provenance, integrity, judge_integrity, provenance) and dc-4 adds a new persistence location (.verdi/diagrams/\u003cname\u003e.sweep-report.md), yet neither decision declares a ratification path through a 02 amendment nor a supersedes/exempts edge against dc-7. Either the new schema is implicitly exempt from the ratify-before-implementation rule (which should be declared) or it must join/extend the amendment batch (which should be stated). (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals, disposition: no-conflict, note: "dc-7's ratify-before-implementation rule targets the DIAGRAM ARTIFACT's own first-class kind-registry schema (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from) — the contract humans author against, whose evolution gates decode/lint of AUTHORED content across the whole corpus, per 02's kind-registry ritual. verdi.diagramsweep/v1 is not that: it is a GENERATED, computed-plus-judged artifact under 02 §Generated artifacts and digests (provenance, digest, integrity discipline) — the same category decision-conflict-report.md (verdi.decisionconflict/v1) and deviation-report.md (verdi.deviation/v1) already occupy, and neither of those two existing precedents went through a dc-7-style pre-ratification amendment before their stories introduced them. 02 §Generated artifacts and digests is itself the sweep report's governing contract, satisfied here by dc-3's reuse of ConflictFinding/JudgeIntegrity/computeIntegrity verbatim. Requiring a NEW ratification step for this report, when no sibling generated-report schema in this codebase has ever needed one, would invent a heavier bar than any precedent — the minimal-invention reading is that dc-7 was never about generated reports at all." }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/judged-sweep#dc-1, spec/judged-sweep#dc-2, spec/judged-sweep#dc-3, spec/judged-sweep#dc-4, spec/judged-sweep#dc-5] }

Story dc-3 mints a new artifact schema (verdi.diagramsweep/v1 with covers, findings, sweep_provenance, integrity, judge_integrity, provenance) and dc-4 adds a new persistence location (.verdi/diagrams/\u003cname\u003e.sweep-report.md), yet neither decision declares a ratification path through a 02 amendment nor a supersedes/exempts edge against dc-7. Either the new schema is implicitly exempt from the ratify-before-implementation rule (which should be declared) or it must join/extend the amendment batch (which should be stated).\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dc7-schema-ratification-bypass** [no-conflict]: Parent dc-7 establishes that the diagram schema surface ratifies as one 02 amendment batch BEFORE implementation (the scoping-canvas round-5.4 pattern), and enumerates that batch's contents — the sweep artifact is not among them. Story dc-3 mints a new artifact schema (verdi.diagramsweep/v1 with covers, findings, sweep_provenance, integrity, judge_integrity, provenance) and dc-4 adds a new persistence location (.verdi/diagrams/<name>.sweep-report.md), yet neither decision declares a ratification path through a 02 amendment nor a supersedes/exempts edge against dc-7. Either the new schema is implicitly exempt from the ratify-before-implementation rule (which should be declared) or it must join/extend the amendment batch (which should be stated). (confidence 0.60) — dc-7's ratify-before-implementation rule targets the DIAGRAM ARTIFACT's own first-class kind-registry schema (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from) — the contract humans author against, whose evolution gates decode/lint of AUTHORED content across the whole corpus, per 02's kind-registry ritual. verdi.diagramsweep/v1 is not that: it is a GENERATED, computed-plus-judged artifact under 02 §Generated artifacts and digests (provenance, digest, integrity discipline) — the same category decision-conflict-report.md (verdi.decisionconflict/v1) and deviation-report.md (verdi.deviation/v1) already occupy, and neither of those two existing precedents went through a dc-7-style pre-ratification amendment before their stories introduced them. 02 §Generated artifacts and digests is itself the sweep report's governing contract, satisfied here by dc-3's reuse of ConflictFinding/JudgeIntegrity/computeIntegrity verbatim. Requiring a NEW ratification step for this report, when no sibling generated-report schema in this codebase has ever needed one, would invent a heavier bar than any precedent — the minimal-invention reading is that dc-7 was never about generated reports at all.
